command: TASK: In THIS worktree only (you are already cd-ed into it), implement ROADMAP.md item 1 (RED-gate exit-code discrimination). CONTEXT: backend/src/app/handlers/execution_handler.py, method _finalize_test_author: after running contract.verification_commands via the verifier, the TDD branch computes valid_baseline = any(item.exit_code != 0), so exit 127 (command not found) or 126 (not executable) from a broken verification command masquerades as a legitimate RED baseline and freezes a test bundle. REQUIREMENT: classify exit codes 126 and 127 (and timeout 124 is already produced by the executor as its own code - leave its existing handling semantics intact) as INFRASTRUCTURE failure, not test failure: when any verification command outcome has exit 126/127, raise TaskFailed with a clear message naming the command and exit code (use the existing failure taxonomy - FailureKind from src/app/runtime_failures.py or the app ports; grep how TaskFailed is raised elsewhere in this handler and follow that pattern; note CommandExecution has no output text, only exit codes, so classification is exit-code based). Apply the same discrimination to the implementer-stage verification path if it has the same blindness, but keep the change minimal. CONSTRAINTS: NEVER modify anything under backend/src/domain/ (frozen domain). Follow existing code style; structlog only, no print. TESTS: add coverage proving a scripted exit-127 verification command surfaces as a distinct infrastructure failure and does NOT freeze a test bundle; follow the existing test patterns in backend/tests/integration/test_tdd_execution.py (which scripts runners and uses LocalVerificationExecutor) and/or the truth-test style in backend/tests/unit/orchestration/. VERIFY before finishing, from backend/: uv run ruff check src tests && uv run mypy src && uv run pytest -q tests/unit tests/integration. All must pass. Commit your work on the current branch (task/red-gate-127/a1) with a conventional commit message. Final message: summarize files changed, test names added, and paste the tail of the pytest summary line.

TASK: In THIS worktree only (your cwd), implement ROADMAP.md item 7 (Consolidate backend/scripts/). Steps: (1) Delete backend/scripts/start_api_and_worker.sh and backend/scripts/reseed_openrouter_key.sh - both are deprecated shims that print notices pointing to dev.sh. (2) Update .github/workflows/development-workflow.yml: remove every reference to the two deleted scripts (the bash -n parse step lists them; the Exercise documented interfaces step calls their --help). Keep all dev.sh checks intact. (3) Grep docs/ and backend/ for any remaining references to the deleted script names and update or remove those mentions (docs/development.md likely references them). (4) Add a documentation header comment to backend/scripts/export_plan_runs.py matching the style/standard of its siblings export_openapi.py and snapshot_current_plan.py (read them first; describe purpose, usage, and that it is stdlib-only reading the orchestrator SQLite DB). Do NOT touch anything under backend/src/ or frontend/. VERIFY: bash -n backend/scripts/dev.sh passes; grep -r start_api_and_worker . --include=*.yml --include=*.md --include=*.sh returns nothing; same for reseed_openrouter_key. Commit on the current branch (task/scripts-consolidation/a1) with a conventional commit message. Final message: list files deleted/modified and the verification grep results.

command: TASK: In THIS worktree only, implement the test-coverage half of ROADMAP.md item 3 (Harden the test foundation). You may ONLY create/modify files under backend/tests/ - do NOT touch backend/src/, frontend/, or workflows. Reconnaissance already established these gaps; close them following the existing patterns in backend/tests/integration/test_api.py (TestClient fixtures, seeded reference data): (1) Routes in backend/src/api/routers/plans.py with zero TestClient coverage: DELETE /{plan_id}/intent, DELETE /{plan_id}/cycle-draft, POST /{plan_id}/review/reopen, POST /{plan_id}/review/finish, POST /{plan_id}/review/replan, POST /{plan_id}/replan, POST /{plan_id}/replanning/message - add happy-path and at least one guard/error assertion each, walking the plan into the right phase via the existing stub-reasoner discovery flow used elsewhere in test_api.py. (2) Error-mapping coverage (src/api/exceptions.py _STATUS_BY_CODE): add assertions on error.code for currently-unasserted codes reachable through the API without contortions - at least GOAL_NOT_FOUND, TASK_NOT_FOUND, AGENT_NOT_FOUND, PROVIDER_NOT_FOUND, STALE_VERSION (409), INVALID_EDIT, PLAN_ALREADY_TERMINAL; skip codes that genuinely cannot be provoked through public routes and say so in your final message. (3) Reference CRUD: cover POST/PUT/DELETE /api/reference/agents and the /api/reference/projects CRUD. (4) SSE: add one test that actually streams GET /api/events with the TestClient (httpx stream), triggers a mutation, and asserts a named event with event_id arrives - see tests/integration/test_outbox_relay.py for the relay setup it complements. Keep tests deterministic - no sleeps; poll bounded. VERIFY from backend/: uv run ruff check tests && uv run pytest -q tests/integration tests/unit - all pass. Commit on branch task/api-test-hardening/a1 with a conventional message. Final message: list test functions added per gap, any codes you could not provoke and why, and the pytest summary line.

TASK: In THIS worktree only, implement the documentation half of ROADMAP.md item 3: make backend/src/app/testing/fakes.py self-documenting. ONLY modify backend/src/app/testing/fakes.py (and backend/src/app/testing/execution_records.py if its fakes also lack docstrings). Add concise class docstrings to the fakes that lack them - InMemoryOutbox, InMemoryUnitOfWork, InMemoryAgentRepository, InMemoryCapabilityRepository, NoOpWorkspace, CollectingEventSink - each stating (a) which REAL adapter it mirrors (read backend/src/infra/db/outbox.py, unit_of_work.py, reference_repos.py, backend/src/infra/git/workspace.py, backend/src/infra/db/agent_event_sink.py first so the claim is accurate) and (b) which real semantics it preserves that tests depend on (e.g. detached-copy aggregates, CAS StaleVersionError shape, lease expiry, transactional rollback discipline) - the module docstring and CLAUDE.md describe these invariants; keep each docstring under 6 lines, match the existing docstring style of FakeClock/InMemoryPlanRepository in the same file. Do NOT change any executable code - docstrings only. VERIFY from backend/: uv run ruff check src && uv run pytest -q tests/unit - must pass. Commit on branch task/fakes-docs/a1 with a conventional message. Final message: list the docstrings added.
